prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_FT_catboost', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.51) and substantial seasonality variance across models, with ARIMA capturing seasonal patterns best while ONLY_FT_catboost leads in overall RMSE and trend tracking. The series exhibits non-stationary behavior with alternating trend directions across folds, making DBA combination the optimal approach to leverage complementary model strengths and handle significant model diversity.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_FT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ARIMA",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_FT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_FT_catboost", "late": "FT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_FT_catboost", "ETS", "THETA", "FT_svr", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["ARIMA", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ONLY_FT_rf", "FT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.5051,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.51) and substantial seasonality variance across models, with ARIMA capturing seasonal patterns best while ONLY_FT_catboost leads in overall RMSE and trend tracking. The series exhibits non-stationary behavior with alternating trend directions across folds, making DBA combination the optimal approach to leverage complementary model strengths and handle significant model diversity."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has good diversity (2 selection, 2 weighted, 1 baseline types). All candidates from valid_candidate_names. No leakage issues detected (baselines never leak, weighted/selection use anti-leakage rolling/expanding per eval_config). Universe leaderboards show current set covers top performers: best_per_horizon_by_validation leads SMAPE/MAPE, dba_combination leads POCID (78.79), inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 and exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0 are strong weighted candidates. No redundancy issues. n_windows=3 is sufficient for weight learning with anti-leakage methods.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["best_single_by_validation", "robust_median"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Added best_single_by_validation (RMSE=8230.48 in universe leaderboard, better than current best_per_horizon_by_validation RMSE=8387.45). Added robust_median to address stability issue (RMSE_std/RMSE=0.4069>0.3). Current set has good diversity with selection/weighted/baseline methods; no removals needed.", "changes": ["added best_single_by_validation for better RMSE in universe", "added robust_median for stability"], "when_good": "When n_windows increases to >=6, consider adding ridge_stacking candidates"}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.51) and substantial seasonality variance across models, with ARIMA capturing seasonal patterns best while ONLY_FT_catboost leads in overall RMSE and trend tracking. The series exhibits non-stationary behavior with alternating trend directions across folds, making DBA combination the optimal approach to leverage complementary model strengths and handle significant model diversity.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates high model diversity requiring per-horizon selection. RMSE spread between ONLY_FT_catboost (8009.58) and CWT_svr (13916.56) is ~73%, favoring top-k over full mean. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling phase shifts with high_seasonality_variance=1.0934. With only n_windows=3, shrinkage≥0.25 stabilizes inverse_rmse_weights. EWA and PWA added as complementary weighted methods.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has good diversity (2 selection, 2 weighted, 1 baseline types). All candidates from valid_candidate_names. No leakage issues detected (baselines never leak, weighted/selection use anti-leakage rolling/expanding per eval_config). Universe leaderboards show current set covers top performers: best_per_horizon_by_validation leads SMAPE/MAPE, dba_combination leads POCID (78.79), inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 and exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0 are strong weighted candidates. No redundancy issues. n_windows=3 is sufficient for weight learning with anti-leakage methods.

statistician.rationale: Added best_single_by_validation (RMSE=8230.48 in universe leaderboard, better than current best_per_horizon_by_validation RMSE=8387.45). Added robust_median to address stability issue (RMSE_std/RMSE=0.4069>0.3). Current set has good diversity with selection/weighted/baseline methods; no removals needed.
changes: added best_single_by_validation for better RMSE in universe
statistician.when_good: When n_windows increases to >=6, consider adding ridge_stacking candidates
